attacking_style: Launches very early in sprint finishes (400m+) after selective, hilly racing has reduced the bunch. Relies on sustained power rather than explosive acceleration in final 200m.
team_tactics: Ineos sets infernal tempo on late climbs/drags to shed pure sprinters, positioning Hayter for reduced bunch sprints. Team commits full resources for 4+ kilometers into finales.
terrain_preference: Rolling and lumpy stages with late drags that eliminate pure sprinters but don't require pure climbing ability. Struggles on flat, technical circuits where positioning is critical.
vulnerability: Positioning errors in technical finals and crashes on hilly parcours. Cannot respond to late accelerations inside final 400m despite being well-positioned. Disappears when leadout fails.
key_patterns: Survives selective hilly finales better than typical sprinters; podiums on lumpy stages (3 observations)
Launches sprint very early (400m+), vulnerable to late accelerations from fresher legs (2 observations)
Heavily dependent on Ineos tempo demolition; ineffective without team setup (3 observations)
Positioning errors costly: crash lost race lead, disappeared when well-positioned for sprint (3 observations)
Broader stage range than pure sprinter but narrower than true classics rider (4 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 Catalunya showed diminished effectiveness — team committed resources but Hayter absent from sprint despite suitable terrain that previously suited him in 2022
